questions.q: On what date did the Jallianwala Bagh massacre take place?
questions.explanation: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred on 13 April 1919.

questions.q: In which city did the Jallianwala Bagh massacre occur?
questions.explanation: The massacre took place at Jallianwala Bagh in the city of Amritsar, Punjab.

questions.q: Which British officer ordered troops to open fire at Jallianwala Bagh?
questions.explanation: Brigadier-General Reginald Dyer ordered the troops to fire on the crowd at Jallianwala Bagh.

questions.q: What was the condition of the crowd gathered at Jallianwala Bagh when firing began?
questions.explanation: The troops opened fire on an unarmed crowd that had gathered in the enclosed garden.

questions.q: Jallianwala Bagh is best described as what kind of place?
questions.explanation: Jallianwala Bagh was an enclosed public garden surrounded by walls and buildings.

questions.q: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre is given which evocative subtitle?
questions.explanation: The massacre is remembered with the subtitle 'The Garden of Sorrow'.

questions.q: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre took place in which province of British India?
questions.explanation: Amritsar and Jallianwala Bagh are in the Punjab province.

questions.q: What effect did the Jallianwala Bagh massacre have on the Indian freedom movement?
options: It intensified the demand for freedom
questions.explanation: The massacre shocked the nation and deepened the demand for freedom from colonial rule.

questions.q: The massacre occurred during the unrest connected with protests against which act?
questions.explanation: The massacre took place amid agitation against the Rowlatt Act in Punjab in 1919.

questions.q: Roughly how many people were killed in the Jallianwala Bagh massacre according to widely cited accounts?
questions.explanation: The firing killed hundreds of people, though exact figures remain disputed.

questions.q: On which Punjabi festival day did large crowds gather at Jallianwala Bagh in 1919?
questions.explanation: Many in the crowd had gathered to mark the festival of Baisakhi on 13 April 1919.

questions.q: Why was escape from Jallianwala Bagh so difficult during the firing?
options: It was on an open plain
The garden had very few narrow exits
questions.explanation: The enclosed garden had only a few narrow exits, which Dyer's troops blocked, trapping the crowd.

questions.q: Many victims at Jallianwala Bagh died after jumping into what within the garden to escape the bullets?
questions.explanation: A number of people died after jumping into a well in the garden to escape the firing.

questions.q: In response to the massacre, Rabindranath Tagore renounced which honour conferred on him by the British?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore renounced his knighthood in protest against the Jallianwala Bagh massacre.

questions.q: What ban had General Dyer imposed in Amritsar shortly before the massacre, which the crowd was defying?
options: A ban on travel by train
questions.explanation: Dyer had proclaimed a ban on public assemblies, and the gathering at the Bagh was deemed to be in defiance of it.

questions.q: Which committee was appointed by the British government to inquire into the Jallianwala Bagh massacre?
questions.explanation: The Hunter Committee, or Hunter Commission, was appointed to investigate the events at Jallianwala Bagh.

questions.q: According to official British figures, how many people were killed at Jallianwala Bagh, a number widely considered an undercount?
questions.explanation: The official British count put the dead at 379, though Indian estimates and the Congress placed the toll far higher.

questions.q: The arrests of which two local leaders had inflamed Amritsar in the days before the massacre?
options: Dr Satyapal and Dr Saifuddin Kitchlew
questions.explanation: The arrest and deportation of Dr Satyapal and Dr Saifuddin Kitchlew had stoked anger in Amritsar before 13 April.

questions.q: After the massacre, the Punjab was placed under what kind of harsh administration?
questions.explanation: Following the massacre and unrest, martial law was imposed across parts of Punjab.

questions.q: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre is widely seen as a turning point that pushed many Indians toward which movement soon after?
questions.explanation: Outrage at the massacre helped propel Indians toward Gandhi's Non-Cooperation Movement of 1920.

questions.q: What humiliating order did Dyer issue in Amritsar that forced Indians to crawl along a particular lane?
questions.explanation: Dyer issued the notorious 'Crawling Order' forcing Indians to crawl down the lane where a missionary had been assaulted.

questions.q: Which Indian leader resigned from the Viceroy's Executive Council in the aftermath of the Punjab atrocities, including Jallianwala Bagh?
questions.explanation: Sir Sankaran Nair resigned from the Viceroy's Executive Council in protest at the Punjab events.

questions.q: Approximately how many rounds of ammunition did Dyer's troops fire at Jallianwala Bagh, by common estimates?
questions.explanation: It is commonly recorded that the troops fired roughly 1,650 rounds into the trapped crowd.

questions.q: Who was the Lieutenant-Governor of Punjab who endorsed the severe repression around the time of the massacre?
questions.explanation: Sir Michael O'Dwyer was the Lieutenant-Governor of Punjab who backed the harsh measures, distinct from General Dyer.

questions.q: In 1940, the revolutionary Udham Singh assassinated which official in London in revenge connected to the Jallianwala Bagh massacre?
questions.explanation: Udham Singh assassinated Sir Michael O'Dwyer in London in 1940, avenging the Punjab atrocities; Dyer had died in 1927.

questions.q: The Indian National Congress set up its own committee to investigate the massacre; on it served which future Congress leader?
questions.explanation: The Congress inquiry committee on the Punjab events included leaders such as Motilal Nehru and Gandhi.

questions.q: After the Hunter Committee censured him, Dyer was relieved of command, but he was lauded by some in Britain who raised what for him?
options: A seat in the Indian council
questions.explanation: Though censured, Dyer was celebrated by some sympathizers in Britain who raised a substantial public fund in his support.

questions.q: Indian estimates of the death toll at Jallianwala Bagh, including those by the Congress, ran to roughly what figure?
questions.explanation: Indian and Congress estimates placed the dead at around a thousand, far above the official figure of 379.

questions.q: Dyer notably did not order what action for the wounded after the firing ceased at Jallianwala Bagh?
questions.explanation: Dyer withdrew his troops without arranging medical aid for the many wounded left in the garden.

questions.q: A national memorial now stands at Jallianwala Bagh; what is it commonly called?
questions.explanation: A memorial featuring the 'Flame of Liberty' was built at Jallianwala Bagh to honour those killed in the massacre.
